narrative_votes: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
CC: Questioning the measurements and science
CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is exaggerated and scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement and a former vice president, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels despite advocating for their reduction.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by implying that predictions of catastrophic consequences have been consistently incorrect and that the issue is being exaggerated.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about the benefits of renewable energy technologies, implying that they will lead to a 'dark and cold future' and are not a viable solution.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: "Renewable energy ... is now the cheapest source of electricity in almost the entire planet," Gore lied.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of Al Gore's statement about renewable energy, implying that it is false and that the science is being misrepresented.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming. This is the goal of the Inflation Reduction Act, a Biden regime initiative that Gore says is "primarily a climate act."
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text implies that there is a hidden plot by powerful groups, in this case the Biden regime, to impose their will on the population under the guise of climate action.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: "rain bombs" and "boiling oceans"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre scare language', indicating a challenge to the motives and integrity of the climate movement.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by stating that none of the predictions made by climate activists have come true, implying that climate change is not as severe as claimed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the scientific consensus on climate change by highlighting the supposed failure of climate predictions, casting doubt on the accuracy of climate science.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement and a former vice president, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuel despite advocating for reduced carbon emissions, challenging his integrity and credibility on climate issues.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for "renewable energy," meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about green technologies by implying that a future reliant on renewable energy would be 'dark and cold', suggesting that such technologies are not viable or desirable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text implies that there is a hidden plot or agenda behind the climate crisis, suggesting that powerful groups are using the issue to push for open borders and potentially undermine national sovereignty.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement by using the term 'climate cultists' and implying that their language is 'bizarre' and scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that past predictions have been incorrect and that the current warnings are part of a long history of unfounded scare-mongering.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the supposed failure of past predictions, implying that the science is unreliable or flawed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, by implying that he is hypocritical in his use of fossil fuels, challenging his integrity and credibility on the issue.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about green technologies, implying that they are not a viable or desirable solution, and that the investment in them will lead to a 'dark and cold future'.
